info.title: Mklinker
info.description: Mklinker is a cross-platform console utility that let's you create multiple symbolic / junction / hard links at once based on a portable config file

commands.name: addlink
commands.description: Adds a new link to config file

options.name: --force
options.description:
(Default: false) If this flag is set it will ignore validation
checks and add it no matter what

options.name: --path
options.description: (Default: linker.config) Specifies path to config file

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: The path to the source file

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: The path to new link file

arguments.name: value
arguments.description:
(Default: Default) The type of link you want to create.
Default is Symbolic for files and Junction for directories

commands.name: addlinks
commands.description: Adds multiple new links at once to config file with optional filtering

options.name: --regex
options.description:
(Default: [\s\S]*) Regex filter deciding which files /
directories to add links for in source folder. It
matches based on file / directory name only. Default
will match everything

options.name: --absolute-regex
options.description:
(Default: [\s\S]*) Additional Regex filter deciding
which files / directories to add links for in source
folder. It matches based on absolute (full) path.
Default will match everything. Both regex filters have
to be matched in order for file / directory to be
added

options.name: --subdirs
options.description:
(Default: false) Determines if files (not directories)
from subdirectories are included as well (recursive)

options.name: --dirs
options.description:
(Default: false) Determines if directory links should
be created instead of file links.

options.name: --path
options.description: (Default: linker.config) Specifies path to config file

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: The path to the source directory

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: The path to the target directory

arguments.name: value
arguments.description:
(Default: Default) The type of link you want to
create. Default is Symbolic for files and Junction for
directories

commands.name: addvar
commands.description: Adds a new variable to config file

options.name: --force
options.description:
(Default: false) If this flag is set it will override existing
variable if name already exists

options.name: --path
options.description: (Default: linker.config) Specifies path to config file

commands.name: config
commands.description: Displays information about config file by default, but can create / delete / modify config file

options.name: --create
options.description: Creates a new config file if it does not exist

options.name: --delete
options.description: Deleted config file if it exists

options.name: --path
options.description: (Default: linker.config) Specifies path to config file

commands.name: interactive
commands.description: Starts an interactive session where you can run multiple commands without 'Mklinker' in front. Use 'exit' to exit from interactive session

options.name: --path
options.description: (Default: linker.config) Specifies path to config file

commands.name: list
commands.description: Lists all the links or variables in the config

options.name: --absolute
options.description:
(Default: false) Will display absolute paths with variables
resolved

options.name: --path
options.description: (Default: linker.config) Specifies path to config file

options.name: --path
options.description: (Default: linker.config) Specifies path to config file

arguments.name: targetPath
arguments.description:
The targetPath matching entry you want to
delete from config.

commands.name: removevar
commands.description: Removes a variable from config file

options.name: --path
options.description: (Default: linker.config) Specifies path to config file

commands.name: scan
commands.description: Detect if circular paths (loops) exist for a given root folder by scanning all directories and collecting data. This detection is very rough and will only give an indication by displaying how often certain directory names are used (indicating loops around those directory names)

options.name: --limit
options.description:
(Default: 30) Maximum amount of subfolders used for detecting
loop (recursion limit). Higher values might result in more
time, but will be more accurate

options.name: --verbose
options.description:
(Default: false) Will display detailed output including every
path that has reached the recursion limit

options.name: --ignore
options.description:
(Default: false) Will ignore folders Mklinker does not have
access to and continue the scan

arguments.name: value
arguments.description:
(Default: .) The root folder that should be scanned. Default
will scan current working directory

commands.name: validate
commands.description: This will validate the config file to see if it is valid

options.name: --all
options.description:
This will list all entries in config and not just the ones that
didn't pass validation

options.name: --path
options.description: (Default: linker.config) Specifies path to config file
